description_long: This is a rectangular glass bottle with a slightly tapered neck, sealed with a red wax or clay cap and wrapped at the top with a white band. The bottle is filled with a dark, organic-looking material, possibly dried plant matter or roots, densely packed inside. A large, aged paper label with handwritten text is affixed to one of the broad faces, giving the bottle an antique or apothecary appearance. The glass is mostly obscured by the contents and the label, but some clear areas are visible at the base and neck. The bottle shows no obvious signs of damage, but the label appears yellowed and worn with age. The overall look suggests it could be a specimen or medicinal bottle from a historical collection.
description: Antique rectangular glass bottle sealed with red wax, filled with dark organic material and featuring a large handwritten label. The bottle has an aged, apothecary-style appearance.
description_view_0: Front view shows the broad face with a large, handwritten label and the red-sealed cap clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the bottle's rectangular profile and the thickness of the dark contents inside.
description_view_2: Opposite broad face with another view of the label, showing more of the bottle's vertical structure and the cap.
description_view_3: Angled rear view reveals the back side, mostly obscured by the dark contents and label edges.
attribute_label_or_branding: Large, aged paper label with handwritten text in brown ink, affixed to the broad face of the bottle. No visible logos or branding.
